title: Lyciodes crenulatum
intro: Lyciodes crenulatum, also known as the Himalayan Sweet Gale, is a plant species that belongs to the family Salicaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine, where it is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lyciodes crenulatum include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Lyciodes crenulatum is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create formulations for respiratory health, such as expectorant and anti-inflammatory remedies.
